title: Nageia elongata
taxonomy: Nageia elongata is classified as a species of coniferous tree in the family Podocarpaceae. It belongs to the genus Nageia and is a member of the phylum Pinophyta. The family Podocarpaceae is a group of coniferous trees and shrubs native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Nageia elongata is also classified as an evergreen tree, meaning it retains its leaves year-round. This classification helps scientists understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Nageia elongata and its place in the plant kingdom.
uses: Nageia elongata is a species of coniferous tree native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. Nageia elongata has been traditionally used in various medicinal practices, with its bark, leaves, and seeds being employed to treat various ailments. The tree's extracts are believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In traditional medicine, Nageia elongata is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions.
